source_pr.title: *: implement index nested loop join
source_pr.body:
When one of join's children is a scan and there are equal conditions that use the index of this scan. We can build this join to a nested loop join and build this scan using index to look up.
Currently index nested loop join is controlled by the hint `/*+ TIDB_INLJ(t1, t2) */` not cost-based.
And only the table in `TIDB_INLJ()` will be considered as outer table of the join.

symptoms: (select a /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t1) */from t1 where a=10 and b=1) union (select /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t2) */ a from t2 where a=11 and b=2) order by a limit 10; (ast/misc_test.go)
failure_signature: (select a /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t1) */from t1 where a=10 and b=1) union (select /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t2) */ a from t2 where a=11 and b=2) order by a limit 10; (ast/misc_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR ('*: implement index nested loop join') primarily changes code behavior and/or updates test expectations, but the diff does not contain a targeted stabilization change tied to a concrete flaky failure signature. We therefore keep a conservative fallback classification.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: evidence: (select a /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t1) */from t1 where a=10 and b=1) union (select /*+ TIDB_SMJ(t2) */ a from t2 where a=11 and b=2) order by a limit 10; (ast/misc_test.go)
